meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no project ideas (Advanced Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no project ideas. A Advanced-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Pricing to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with project ideas. Learn to build advanced SaaS pricing models using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your first portfolio-ready app.
content.answer_block: The 'no project ideas' trap is solved by engineering a complex SaaS pricing engine. By treating billing as a technical feature—handling tiered subscriptions, usage-based metering, and role-based access control—you transform a blank screen into a sophisticated, portfolio-grade system that demonstrates high-level architecture skills and production-ready code.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank IDE, the cursor blinking with rhythmic indifference. You have the technical skills to code, but you're paralyzed by the 'what.' Most students fall into the trap of building another To-Do list, but that won't land you a job. The secret to a standout portfolio isn't a novel idea; it's the depth of your infrastructure.

Today, we pivot. We aren't just building a feature; we are building a robust SaaS pricing engine. By focusing on the logic of recurring revenue, subscription tiers, and Stripe integration, you’ll solve the 'no project' problem forever. This is where engineering meets business, and it is exactly what hiring managers want to see.

steps.title: Define the Tiered Architecture
steps.description: Establish the data schema for three distinct pricing tiers (Free, Pro, Enterprise) with unique feature flags and usage limits.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a TypeScript interface for a SaaS subscription model including tiered feature flags, usage quotas, and monthly/annual pricing cycles.
steps.claude_code_command: claude create --file types/subscription.ts --prompt 'Define a robust interface for a SaaS pricing model with feature flags'

steps.title: Implement Stripe Webhook Handlers
steps.description: Securely manage subscription events to ensure user access levels sync with their payment status.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a secure Express.js webhook handler for Stripe events: checkout.session.completed and invoice.payment_succeeded.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --file src/webhooks/stripe.ts --context 'Handle Stripe subscription lifecycle events'

steps.title: Build Usage-Based Metering Logic
steps.description: Create a service to track user actions against their tier limits to trigger upgrade prompts.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a middleware service that checks user usage against their subscription tier quota and throws a 403 error if exceeded.
steps.claude_code_command: claude refactor --file src/middleware/quotaGuard.ts --logic 'enforce tier-based usage limits'

steps.title: Frontend Pricing Table Component
steps.description: Develop a responsive, high-converting pricing toggle component using Tailwind CSS.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a React component for a pricing table with a monthly/annual toggle, highlighting the 'Pro' tier using Tailwind.

steps.title: Unit Testing the Billing Logic
steps.description: Verify that your pricing engine handles edge cases like mid-cycle upgrades and plan cancellations.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write Jest test cases to simulate subscription upgrades and verify that access flags update in the database correctly.

pro_tips: Always implement 'grace periods' in your subscription logic to prevent immediate lockout during payment retries.
Use Feature Flags (like LaunchDarkly or custom logic) to decouple your code deployment from business plan changes.
Never store pricing data in the frontend; always validate the subscription state on the server before granting access to API endpoints.
Implement 'Stripe Checkout' sessions rather than building custom credit card forms to ensure PCI compliance without the headache.
Add a 'usage-warning' event to your database that triggers when a user hits 80% of their quota to improve UX and conversion.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing engine is live, deploy your app to Vercel and add a 'Pricing' section to your portfolio site that explains the technical challenges you solved during this build.
content.conversion_block: Stop building toy apps and start building systems that solve real business problems. You have the tools, the roadmap, and the expertise. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
